caustica.options.rt.exposureMode.tooltip: How the ray-traced image is mapped to display brightness. Auto adapts to scene luminance; Manual uses the exposure value directly.
caustica.options.rt.manualEv.tooltip: Exposure compensation in stops. In Manual, this is the fixed exposure. In Auto, this biases the auto exposure brighter or darker.
caustica.options.rt.spp.tooltip: Path-tracing samples taken per pixel each frame. Higher is cleaner but more demanding.
caustica.options.rt.maxBounces.tooltip: Maximum number of secondary path-tracing bounces after the primary hit. Higher captures more indirect light but costs more.
caustica.options.rt.sunSize.tooltip: Angular radius of the sun and moon disk. Larger discs cast softer shadow penumbrae.
caustica.options.rt.entities.tooltip: Include entities and block entities in the ray-traced scene.
caustica.options.rt.particles.tooltip: Draw particles as camera-ray billboards inside the traced scene.
caustica.options.rt.glow.tooltip: Draw the vanilla Glowing-effect outline (through walls) around glowing entities.
caustica.options.rt.waterWaves.tooltip: Animate water-surface normals for moving wave highlights.
caustica.options.rt.dlssRr.tooltip: AI denoiser + upscaler. Off = raw noisy path trace with 1:1 blit. Toggle to compare noise vs. blur on transparent surfaces (water, glass).
caustica.options.rt.hdr.tooltip: Present in HDR (ST.2084/PQ) instead of SDR, when the display supports it. Takes effect after restarting the game.
caustica.options.rt.hdrPaperWhite.toolti: Absolute brightness (in nits) that SDR-equivalent scene brightness maps to on an HDR display.
caustica.options.rt.hdrPeak.tooltip: Absolute brightness (in nits) highlights roll off toward. Set to your display's peak HDR brightness.
caustica.options.rt.bloom.tooltip: Adds a soft glow around bright light sources in the ray-traced image.
caustica.options.rt.bloomIntensity.toolt: Strength of the bloom glow. 0 disables the visual effect without turning off the passes.
caustica.options.rt.bloomThreshold.toolt: Luminance level (in paper-white units) above which pixels start to bloom. Lower values bloom more of the scene.
caustica.options.rt.bloomKnee.tooltip: Log2 stop width of the soft threshold transition. 0 = hard step, 6 = very gradual shoulder. Works in perceptually uniform stops (1 stop = 2× luminance).
caustica.options.rt.bloomRadius.tooltip: Controls the depth of the bloom pyramid (0=tight, 6=screen-wide diffusion). Each level adds a wider, softer glow tier with 13-tap tent-filtered blur.
caustica.options.rt.waterAbsorptionR.too: Water red-channel absorption per metre. Higher = red light absorbed faster = bluer water. 0 = crystal clear, 100% = ink.
caustica.options.rt.waterAbsorptionG.too: Water green-channel absorption per metre. Higher = green light absorbed faster = magentier water. 0 = crystal clear, 100% = ink.
caustica.options.rt.waterAbsorptionB.too: Water blue-channel absorption per metre. Higher = blue light absorbed faster = yellower/greener water. 0 = crystal clear, 100% = ink.
caustica.options.rt.swamp.absorptionR.to: Swamp water red-channel absorption per metre. Higher = red light absorbed faster. 0 = crystal clear, 100% = ink.
caustica.options.rt.swamp.absorptionG.to: Swamp water green-channel absorption per metre. Higher = green light absorbed faster. 0 = crystal clear, 100% = ink.
caustica.options.rt.swamp.absorptionB.to: Swamp water blue-channel absorption per metre. Higher = blue light absorbed faster = yellower/greener swamp water. 0 = crystal clear, 100% = ink.
caustica.options.rt.dlssQuality.tooltip: DLSS Ray Reconstruction quality mode. Lower-quality modes render fewer pixels and upscale more aggressively for higher framerates; higher-quality modes render more pixels for a sharper image. Only affects the image when DLSS Ray Reconstruction is enabled.
caustica.options.rt.tonemapContrast.tool: AgX look contrast. Pivots around mid-grey. Above 100% deepens shadows and brightens highlights.
caustica.options.rt.tonemapSaturation.to: Global colour saturation. 0 = greyscale, 100% = neutral, 200% = vivid.
caustica.options.rt.tonemapTemperature.t: White-balance shift. Positive = warmer (amber), negative = cooler (blue).
caustica.options.rt.tonemapVibrance.tool: Smart saturation boost. Stronger on muted colours, leaves already-vivid colours alone.
caustica.options.rt.debugView.tooltip: Visualize an intermediate ray-tracing buffer instead of the final image.
